desc:
The entrance is leading to a courtyard northwards, and there are hallways both
east and west of here. All the walls and the roof are made of stone, and some
old weapons and shields are hanging on the walls above the hallways. Some
strange sounds, like men moving around, can be heard above. The ground is
covered with old cobblestones. Heavy usage of horses and men, have worn out the
stones.
name: Inside the Gates

desc:
The room is pretty dark because of the rather small opening westwards, and a
door northwards that lets no light through. The roof is made of solid stone, as
is the floor. The floor seems less used here than west of here, probably because
no mounts and pets have been allowed here. Silence fills the room, as nothing
can be heard in any direction due to the heavy door northwards, and the low
opening westwards.
name: East of the Gates

desc:
This ancient hallway contains more luxury and the carvings are more detailed
than the other rooms in the eastern parts of the castle. North of here an
ancient door blocks the way. The door is solid and some carvings can be seen on
it. In the south-western corner of the room a stair leads upwards to a tower
seen above. Some lanterns hang on the walls around the room, and they give off
enough light so that each corner of the room is lit.
name: Hallway of Minas Brethil

desc:
This is a large room, which contains a big table with a dozen chairs around it.
The table stands in one end of the room, while the rest of the room seems rather
empty, besides some old maps and drawings on the walls. The walls are made of
brick like stones, still the room is warm and comfortable. One of the reasons
for that might be the wooden floor.
name: War Room

desc:
This is where the guards of the castle live and spend their time when they have
no assignment. When not on duty the guards occupy themselves with games or
attending to their accoutrements. The men each have a bed and locker for their
belongings including their armour weapons and uniforms, but precious little
else. In addition to lodgings, it contains rooms for meals and training.
name: Guard Barracks

desc:
A large squarelike area that clearly shows indications of heavy use. The sturdy
cobblestones worn by both the weather and the frequent visits of many people. In
the centre a large grey fountain can be seen. Its clear water flowing steadily.
The massive front gates and their overgate structure loom above the southern
portion. To the east lies the old and well used stables while north of here the
exit to the Great Halls can be seen. West of here the lies a large brick
building, housing the guards of the castle.
name: Courtyard

desc:
Placed on the eastern portion of the Courtyard lies the wood building that
houses the mounts and pets of the inhabitants. Made out of thick pines and with
a thatched roof it makes a safe lodging. In connection to the stable a small
paddock can be seen, where the mounts get their daily exercise. The keeper of
this lodging is the Grumm family. A family that has outlasted many of the noble
lines of Rhudaur.
name: Stables

desc:
This hallway is light, as it is lit up by several lanterns on each wall. South
of here a huge door blocks the entrance, while a hall appears to the north. The
stones used to make the walls here are not as dark as in the rest of the castle,
which indicates that this part was finished at a latter stage. A carpet covers
the floor which is made of stone as in the rest of the castle. The temperature
here is rather cold.
name: The Ancient Hallway

desc:
Carved from an anomalous lobe of maroon-streaked crimson stone, the hall
resembles a chapel of retrospection. Its walls, alcoves, pilasters, and arching
vault are carved in mannish forms whose faces resembles the old rulers of
Rhudaur. The floor is covered with a thick red carpet. Upon this carpet stands a
large roundish oaktable. Here the valiant heroes of Rhudaur have their meetings,
discussing different news from the world outside. Around on the walls different
signs can be seen. The entrance is secured by a large oakdoor, covered with
detailed carvings.
name: The Grand Hall of Red

desc:
Like the western tower this one has the same openings high on the wall. The
openings let some light in so the castle is fairly lit. In addition to this some
lanterns are placed around in the tower so it can be lit at night. An opening
westwards leads to the battlements above the gate, and a stairway, leading to
the top of the tower, can be seen north of here.
name: Eastern Tower

desc:
The stairway leads upward, toward the top of the tower. Despite the long stairs
this chamber seems rather empty. The only thing of interest that can be seen
here is a board, mounted upon the wall. The walls themselves are constructed
with brick-like stones, greyish in colour. To the south the main floor of the
eastern tower can be seen, through an arched doorway, and through the small
square window in the west wall, the castle grounds spread out below.
name: Eastern Stairway

desc:
The cave contains several signs that tell about the background of the
inhabitants. They tell about the place of roaring red gold, of its history,
inhabitants, famous places, and more. The cave itself is cold, and nothing
covers the floor. Lanterns are the only light source, giving out a dim light.
Due to its round form, the cave has no corners. South of here a larger hall can
be seen, and you notice some light from a fireplace.
name: The Cave of History

desc:
This huge hall has several lanterns mounted on the walls, and a fireplace gives
off some light. The hall is about ten times thirty meters in size and made of
stone. The fireplace provides necessary heating. Around on the walls several
signs tells stories from battles the inhabitants participated in. Some pictures
reconstructing the battles can also be seen on the western wall. North of here a
small cave appears, while a hallway leads southwards.
name: The Hall of Ancient Tales

desc:
The height between the floor and the roof is quite low here, not more than six
feet at the most. The floor is made of wood, which squeaks as you move across
the room. In one corner stands a desk and a chair, which have obviously been
used recently, and small square windows set in each wall offer an excellent view
of the castle and its surroundings. A narrow stairway leads to the main floor of
the tower, below.
name: Top of the Eastern Tower

desc:
From here it's possible to see down on the path and the wooden bridge in front
of the castle. Below on the other side lies the courtyard, and noise of people
and horses can be heard. The battlements are made of huge brick like stones,
while the floor is made of wood. Eagles are circling above, looking for small
animals in the dense vegetation around the castle. Towers can be seen both east
and west of here.
name: On the Battlements

desc:
The tower contains a few small square-like openings high on the wall. They are
high up on the wall so that it is impossible to see out through them. The walls
are made of stones formed like bricks. East of here there is an opening that
leads out on some battlements. Further east another tower like this one can be
seen. North of here a stairway can be seen, leading upwards to the top of the
tower. Stairs lead downwards to the lower parts of the castle.
name: Western Tower

desc:
The stairway leads upward, offering access to the top floor of the tower. There
is almost nothing in this chamber, save for a heavy board, which is mounted to
one of the grey stone walls. Through an elegantly arched doorway, to the south,
the main floor of the tower can be seen, and through the small square window in
the east wall, the castle grounds can be spied below.
name: Western Stairway

desc:
From this lofty room it is possible to see much of Fornost and Fornost Erain.
Looking downward through the small square windows affords a view of the castle
courtyard to the east, and of the castle surroundings. The chamber is low-
roofed, such that anyone more than about six feet in height would have to stoop
to avoid banging their head on the ceiling, and the only way out of here is via
a narrow stairway, leading downward. The room contains only a simple bed, a
small desk, and two old chairs.
name: Top of the Western Tower

desc:
This room has been built from sturdy dark stone. The rock has been cut out and
brought here from the granite filled mountains of Rhudaur. Neatly fitted and
polished with great care its difficult to see that the room has been made by
seperate stones. From this hall several doors can be seen, leading to the
private rooms of the castle inhabitants. No hangings or storied webs, nor any
things of woven stuff or wood can be seen in this solemn room.
name: Quarters

desc:
The small cobbled yard is surrounded by walls on all sides. Some soldiers are
perfecting their skills at swordplay under the supervision of a higher officer.
An archery range has been constructed towards the south wall, for the Dunadan
scouts and hunters to practice at. North is a gate leading out of the garrison,
and east the headquarters of Fornost's forces.
name: The Exercise Yard

desc:
This staircase leads in winding steps up the tower beside the main gate of the
city. Although the steps are relatively wide, they are also quite old and worn
out, which makes them slippery. There is nothing to hold on to, so watch your
step!
name: The Staircase

desc:
This is where many of the soldiers keep themselves occupied when they are not on
duty. Some of them play cards, some read while others just chat. A huge wooden
table dominates the room and several chairs are set around it. The room has no
windows, but there are a couple of paintings of famous battles. On one of them
you see the hordes of Angmar storming a city which might be Fornost. You can't
be sure though, because all you can see is a part of a city wall.
name: The Soldiers Quarters

desc:
This is a huge sleeping room with 15 two story beds. Cupboards for the private
possessions of the soldiers are along the walls and paintings of more or less
dressed women are attached to them.
name: Sleeping Room

desc:
You have entered a small prison cell just big enough for two prisoners. You see
two plank beds and a wooden desk with a chair in front of it. In a corner of the
room there is a shelf and a small, dirty mirror. The window is safeguarded with
heavy iron bars. The only exit is a metal door to the north.
name: The Prison Cell

desc:
You enter a small but neat and luxuriously furnished room. A red carpet covers
the floor, which creaks as you move over it. Small windows high on the northern
wall allows light to slip in on sunny days. The largest part of the room is
occupied by an old, heavy desk make of oak, covered with papers and maps. There
are also some comfortable chairs and a small cabinet containing liqueur in fine
bottles.
name: The Commandant's Office

desc:
The corridor widens up a bit, and you see a small niche with a desk in it. To
the east you see a very comfortably furnished office, and in contrast with this
there is a solid metal door on the southern wall - obviously leading to the
jail. A sign sits on the secretary's table, and a notice hangs beside it.
name: The Corridor

desc:
You walk through a plain, simple corridor that runs east and west. There are
small windows high on the southern wall, but they don't let much light in, so
there are burning torches attached to the wall every here and there. A door is
in the northern wall.
name: The Corridor

desc:
This must be one of the quieter places on Craftsmen's Alley. Not that it means
this place is peaceful: the many voices of the street-mongers are replaced by
the noise created as metal hits metal, coming from a smithy north of the street.
Thick, black smoke rises from its chimney.
name: Craftsmen's Alley

desc:
You arrive this old and crumbling gateway, yet another monument of the city's
past. It bridges the northern side of the street with the southern, where the
city's garrison is nowadays. Wherever it once led, whatever building it once was
the entrance to, is now long gone and forgotten.
name: At an Old Gateway

desc:
This street shines out in contrast to its dingy neighbourhood, like a fire in a
forest; and with its freshly painted shutters, well-polished brasses, and
general cleanliness and gaiety, it instantly catches and pleases the eye of the
beholder.
name: Wall Street

desc:
The street runs north and south along the city's eastern wall. You can see
rangers patrolling the battlements above you while narrow, high houses line the
other side of the road. To the north lies the heart of the city, the Market
Square.
name: Wall Street

desc:
You are standing in the shadow of the high city walls, below one of the few
sections that hasn't needed any rebuilding. The scent of freshly baked bread
grabs hold of your head and turns it north, where you discover a tiny bakery.
name: Eastern End of Market Square

desc:
You are standing inside a small bakery. A sweet scent of freshly baked bread
fills the room. You feel hungry as you look at all these fine breads well
arranged on their shelves. A tidy sign is nailed to the wall.
name: Small Bakery

desc:
You have entered a big hall with a great deal of activity. People are constantly
coming in, doing their business, and leaving thereafter. You can check here if
somebody wrote a letter to you, or write one yourself to your friends in other
cities far away.
name: Post Office

desc:
This is the place where you can buy excellent weapons, new or used, as you like,
in every price range. The weaponsmith will also mend your old sword if you are a
little low on cash right now. A large sign is pinned on the wall behind the
counter.
name: The Mighty Hammer Weaponry

desc:
You stand at an old well, which apparently still serves its purpose, as people
arrive to fill their barrels, pots or waterskins. You hear laughter and singing
through a window in house to the south, but you cannot see a thing, for the
curtains are closed, and the entrance is a bit west, under a bright red sign.
name: At the Well

desc:
Fornost's citizens don't seem like a very religious lot to you, but of course
they don't totally ignore their creators. This circular temple contain statues
and portraits of the Valar and the Maiar, but also men and elves the citizens
feel worthy of such an honour. A board here contains all known prophesies and
commandments the creators have laid down on the shoulders of mankind.
name: The Circular Temple

desc:
The footpath you are standing on takes you to a small, round sanctuary if you
continue east on it. The city wall is just north and houses block your way
south. Turning your attention to the gardens for a moment, it seems like the
gardener is a true master of his profession. The lawns are all very green and
neat, the various bushes well trimmed, and the flowers have just the right
colours. Clearly a case of quality before quantity!
name: The Small, Well-tended Gardens

desc:
This room is small, but the lack of clutter makes it appear more spacious. On
the wall several maps of the nearby forests hang. A table stands in the centre
of the room. A single door leads out to the south.
name: Scout Trainer's Room

desc:
Placed beneath the earths surface its air is cold and fresh. This is where most
of the castle supplies and spare equipment is stored. Rows upon rows of shelves
store every conceivable thing the inhabitants and their servers might need. The
room is made out of the same stone as found in the Quarters. The black stone
seems to absorb most of the light brought in here, making it impossible to
distinguish shadows of any kind.
name: Storage Room

desc:
Well hidden beyond the Storage Room this is one of the last resorts to get away
from the castle should it be attacked. Made partly of rock partly of wood the
small corridor leads southwards. Near the end of the corridor barly noticable a
small crack leads upward. Its made in such a way that once left there is no way
to get back in.
name: Hidden Passage

desc:
The staircase ends here as it reaches its top at a small landing. There is a
doorway on the western wall, and a ladder which leads up to the top of the
tower.
name: The Staircase

desc:
You are standing on the top of this old, crumbling tower. Like everything else
in Fornost, it has been heavily patched, so you proceed with caution as you lean
on the battlement and adore the splendid view of Fornost and its outskirts. This
would make a great spot to watch anyone moving on the road that leads south from
Fornost. A high cliff with a castle on its top blocks your view westwards.
name: Tower at the Gate

desc:
You stand in a room which contains lots of strange machinery, some winches and a
fireplace with a big pile of dry wood beside it. By the size of this fireplace
you can tell it is not made to keep the soldiers warm and dry, but rather to
warm up a large pot containing a thick fluid, probably oil of some kind. The pot
is mounted on an axis which allows it to be tilted, so its contents can be
poured down through holes in the floor. You look through these holes and see the
passageway between the gate and the portcullis. An old, worn book has been
thrown away in a corner.
name: Above the Gate of Fornost

desc:
As you walk past the guard's post and into the dimly lit room, you quickly
notice an unusual odour. A rather grandiose fireplace occupies the centre of the
west wall while the assortment of chairs around the fireplace would seem to
accomodate virtually any guest. On the south wall, a large portrait is proudly
displayed between two fine tapestries.
name: Ticar's Home

desc:
As you enter the room, your suspicions of the source of the unusual odour are
confirmed as your olefactory senses are overwhelmed. The small room is obviously
where Ticar practices and perfects his magical mastery. Books line the north and
east walls with an occasional scroll folded between the books. On the south
wall, several shelves hold some small pouches and an occasional potion. In the
southwest corner, a large sack hangs from the ceiling by a rope. Nestled in the
northeast corner is a small table with a stool shoved under it. An unusual
spherical crystal is perched upon the table. A cauldron precariously occupies
the southeast corner.
name: Alchemy Laboratory

desc:
After squeezing by the small iron gate, the tranquility of the very private
courtyard immediately puts you at ease. The courtyard is surrounded by an
enourmous stone wall that separates the bustling capital city from Ticar's own
private garden. The wall is consumed by an ivy that completely covers it. To the
west, two benches large enough for two people each sit facing each other near
the wall and under a large oak tree. To the southeast, a large flat stone sits
on top of a dirt mound. In the centre of the courtyard, a statue of a petite
elven child is surrounded on three sides by small rose bushes. As you walk
closer, you notice that the child is the centrepiece of a fountain and a small
stream of water flows from the child's mouth. The scent of honeysuckle
tantilizes your senses as you stand in awe of this beautiful setting. Nestled
under the eve of the house in the northeast corner of the courtyard you notice a
small hut and next to the entrance to the hut stands a proud suit of armour.
name: The Courtyard

desc:
Sacks of flour, crates of dried cured beef, and baskets of vegetables and
potatoes are stacked along the left wall of this room. To the right stands a
small but well-used food preparation table, a sink, and a wood stove. Clearly,
this is where the master of the house prepares his rations for his long hunts,
as well as his home cooked dinners. Hanging on hooks from the ceiling are
various herbs and plants being dried for use in herbal remedies.
name: Crowded Pantry

desc:
An immense four post bed with a huge feather-stuffed comforter dominates this
room. Its large posts have been cut from oak and appear to be small trees
themselves. A huge oak dresser stands next to a granite wall, and a huge white
pelt of a cavebear serves as a rug on the stone floor. Torch sconces are placed
into the walls, and the stone above them is blackened by their soot. A window,
draped in red velvet, looks out onto the street. Above the bed, a portrait hangs
on the wall.
name: Master Bedroom

desc:
In this small office is a rather large round table surround by eight wooden
chairs. The table, like the walls, is covered in an assortment of maps and land
surveys. Markers on one of the larger table maps show which parcels of land are
in use and what is available for future construction.
name: City Planning Office

desc:
The walls of this passage are made of large stone blocks placed one upon
another. The blocks are piled from the floor to the ceiling where they form a
dome. Inside the dome, bricks of dark and light marble create a intricate
pattern across the ceiling. Along the walls of stone, majestic statues carved in
the same marble seen in the ceiling contribute to the feeling of greatness the
owner must have intended when building this hall.
name: A Stone Hall

desc:
After climbing through the secret entrance, you have come across a foyer. This
room is rather spartan and is devoid of anything of interest. The white door to
the north leads into another room, while you may leave via the grate.
name: Foyer

desc:
Upon entering this room, subtle scent of mauve petals permeates the air. The
beautiful aroma originates from a bowl, which is half-full with mythical mauve
petals. The bowl lies on a large table made of cedar and next to it lies a bone
pipe. Rest of the table is covered with notes and manuscripts, which seem to be
of arcaic nature. Large bookcases line the northern wall and a very comfortable
looking armchair is placed, between the bookcases and the table, within easy
arm's reach from the petals and the books. There is an oakdoor to the west,
leading into the bedroom and passage to the south leads to the entrance of this
simple house.
name: Study

desc:
This room is rather spartan, consisting of a mere bed and a chest at the foot of
it. It seems that the owner of this house is a man of simplistic pleasure.
name: Bedroom

desc:
This is a loud place! You hear harsh voices, shrill laughter, and the high sound
of glasses clinking. An endless stream of beer finds its way into the bellies of
the rough men who frequent this room. The swords are removed, however, and it is
usually very peaceful here. A sign hangs on one of the walls.
name: Dining Hall of the Crown

desc:
You are standing on the Market Square, right outside the recently opened post
office. In the same building, but west some, you can see the city's council. The
'Crown of Arnor' Inn is south of the square, and between it and you there is a
well. In the east you can spot a section of the high city wall.
name: Market Square

desc:
Surrounding you are herbs of such variety and diversity that you are in awe.
Spices and leaves, teas and beans, they fill each nook and cranny. Behind the
counter hangs a sign.
name: Herb Shop

desc:
You are standing in the large entrance hall to the soldier quarters. This is the
place where the daily briefings are held. The room has something cold and
businesslike about it, and the floor has been waxed not long ago. A corridor
leads east to the commandants office and the prison cell.
name: Entrance Hall

desc:
This glade is surrounded by large gnarled trees blocking out most of the natural
light. In the larger patches of light grow many red roses that sing with an
unfamiliar beauty. Near the middle of the glade, there is a small courtyard
paved with black marble stones. The centre of the courtyard is dominated by a
fountain made from a strange black stone that seems to absorb the light around
it lending an erie quality to the scene. Surounding this courtyard are several
obsidian statues that have been carved into figures of the fallen kings of
Gondor. On one side, a bronze plinth sits empty, awaiting the final statue. Some
letters are carved in the plinth. To the east there is a massive marble
staircase leading up to black steel-banded ghostwood door set into the western
face of an enormous tower. To the west lies an archway with a twisted iron gate
leading out into the bustling city beyond.
name: Dreary Glade

desc:
The base of the tower here has a small landing with a narrow set of grey stone
stairs that lead upward in a spiral around the inside of the tower. Despite the
many years of service, the stones do not show any wear from use. To the west is
a steel banded door.
name: Ascending Stairway

desc:
There is a small archway set into the eastern side of the city wall, with a
stony path leading out from it. Just over the wall, a few crumbling parapets are
barely visible. The street continues to the south, while a large square
stretches out west and north.
name: Eastern End of Market Square

desc:
A stone wall lining the path on either side provides shelter from all but the
noon day sun. Vines and roots have covered the walls in a carpet of dense
vegetation. The path itself is laid with stone, well worn by the tread of hoof
and boot.
name: Narrow Path

desc:
The stony path gives way to a wooden bridge here. It is fairly small, but built
solid, clearly strong enough for several men on horseback. A small river of
crystal clear water runs silently under the bridge. An ancient castle can be
seen to the north, while to the west the path continues through the walls of
Fornost.
name: Wooden Bridge

desc:
A huge gate towers up ahead, made of ancient beech wood. A sign is posted on one
gate, with neat gold lettering upon dark wood. The castle itself is made of
stone, and looks dark and fearsome, yet inviting. Dense vegetation abounds in
the area, making the castle hard to see from a distance, although the walls
stand about eighteen feet high. Towers dominate each corner of the castle, and
movements can be seen on the battlements high above you.
name: Before the Gates of Minas Brethil

desc:
The walls on both the west and east side of this medium-sized chamber are made
of solid stone, except on the north end where the straight walls begin to curve.
The curved section of the wall was made with a beautiful white marble, with
black and silver specks throughout each block drawing attention. A fine silver
has been placed along the top and bottom of the walls as trim, with a unique
pattern that trails from the south end to the marble portion, where two argent
pillars stand on each side. Between the pillars is a silver-framed painting of a
royal figure, with a silver plaque of inscription fastened above it. An
expansive mural was painted on each of the stone walls leading to the pillars;
one of a shipwreck upon the frozen seas and the other appears to be an immense
battle with the Witch-king from an age long passed. The floor is polished and
white, with the ceiling above matching, causing any noise to echo through the
chamber.
name: Arvedui's Memoir Chamber

desc:
Massive oak timbers frame the interior of this private home. Large glass lamps
hang from the high vaulted ceiling on long iron chains. The far side of the
lodge is dominated by a massive fireplace with an immense mantle hewn from oak
and stained pitch black from the soot of countless fires. All along the granite
walls are large storage shelves and hanging pegs that hold a wide variety of
hunting gear, stable tack, and implements of war. A spiral stone staircase leads
to the rooms above.
name: A Hunting Lodge
